title: Parking will be free at state parks, beaches for Fourth of July weekend
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts will waive parking fees at state parks and beaches from July 3 to July 5 for the Fourth of July.
The free parking initiative commemorates the 250th anniversary of the American Revolution, announced by Governor Maura Healey.
High-demand areas may close if parking lots are full; visitors are encouraged to check the DCR Park Alerts webpage for updates.

title: What’s up with those online ads for gas-saving gadgets?
author: John Paul
bullets: Online ads for gas-saving gadgets are often unverified and ineffective, according to AAA Northeast's Car Doctor, John Paul.
Run-flat tires have improved but still provide a harsher ride than conventional tires, according to automotive expert John Paul.
A 2002 Honda Accord with an old timing belt should have it replaced soon, despite low mileage, advises John Paul.

title: Boston civilian board recommends changes to BPD policies on bodycam footage, federal entanglements
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's Office of Police Accountability and Transparency recommends BPD release bodycam footage of critical incidents within 30 days.
OPAT urges Boston Police Department to separate from federal agencies, including FBI and DHS, to protect community trust.
Recommendations include prohibiting sharing of non-felony arrestees' biometric data outside BPD without supervisory authorization.

title: Senators seek to restrict social media use for minors, target ‘most addictive features’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts Senate proposes to disable 'addictive' social media features for minors like autoplay and infinite scroll.
Senate bill avoids using government-issued ID for age verification, tasking the Attorney General with setting verification methods.
Legislation, pushed forward by Massachusetts Senate, received criticism for potential impacts on LGBTQ+ and marginalized communities.

title: Isaiah Thomas comes to Jaylen Brown’s defense after shocking Celtics trade​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was traded from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and draft picks.
Isaiah Thomas defended Jaylen Brown's character on social media amid negative commentary following the trade.
Brown expressed shock at the trade, reflecting on his time and achievements with the Boston Celtics.

title: Jeremiah Poope takes us on the Freedom Trail for America’s 250th (yes, that’s really his name)
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Jeremiah Poope, a 23-year veteran tour guide, led a Freedom Trail tour in Boston during a heatwave, wearing full period costume.
Poope chose not to embody a historical character, instead sharing personal anecdotes and folklore with the tour groups.
The Freedom Trail Foundation allows guides to skip costumes in heat, but Poope wore his to enhance tourist experience.

title: 60 cars burn in fire at Mass. auto auction site
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Fire destroyed 50 to 60 cars at Insurance Auto Auctions in Templeton early Thursday morning.
Firefighters responded around 2 a.m. and controlled the blaze by approximately 6 a.m.
No injuries were reported, and the incident is under investigation by authorities.

title: Boston hit 100. See what meteorologists are saying about chances for triple digits Friday and the July 4 forecast.
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Boston recorded 100°F on Thursday, marking the 29th occurrence on record, breaking a daily high from 1963.
Meteorologists predict continuing heat with potentially 100°F temperatures on Friday, posing dangerous heat and humidity conditions.
Severe thunderstorms forecast for Friday could bring damaging winds, especially in western Massachusetts and Connecticut.

title: Windhorst: Celtics didn’t think Jaylen Brown was their best player last season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics reportedly valued Derrick White's performance higher than Jaylen Brown's during the 2025-26 season.
Jaylen Brown posted career highs in points, rebounds, and assists, leading the Celtics to a 56-26 record.
Boston traded Brown to the 76ers for Paul George and draft picks amid mixed opinions on his value.

title: ‘I love you JB’: 6-year-old Celtics fan’s tearful message to Jaylen Brown goes viral
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Jaylen Brown's trade to the Philadelphia 76ers prompted a tearful reaction from a 6-year-old Celtics fan.
The fan, Gio, expressed his love for Brown in a viral video, gaining over 840,000 likes on Instagram.
Jaylen Brown responded to Gio's video with a comforting comment, saying 'It's ok lil bro 499 we will always be friends.'
